_meta.program: Computer Science Bachelor of Science (Qatar University, catalog year 2024)
_meta.source: Converted from student-provided QU CS BSc catalog export.
_meta.notes: One-directional corequisites are merged into 'prerequisites' (simplified to 'must be done before enrolling'). TRUE mutual corequisite pairs (lecture/lab pairs where each lists the other as a corequisite) are kept separate under 'symmetric_corequisites' and scheduled as a bundle by planner.py/simulation.py, since merging them into prerequisites would create an unsatisfiable cycle. Original prerequisite/corequisite split is preserved per-course under 'source_prerequisites'/'source_corequisites'. 'description', 'requires_room_type', and 'equipment_needed' were inferred (not sourced from the official catalog) to support the resource-planning and new-elective-feasibility engines - review and correct any that are wrong.

CMPS151.name: Programming Concepts
CMPS151.description: Introduces fundamental programming concepts: variables, control flow, functions, and basic problem-solving using a high-level language.

CMPS251.name: Object-Oriented Programming
CMPS251.description: Object-oriented programming principles: classes, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, and design patterns, building on introductory programming.

CMPS200.name: Computer Ethics
CMPS200.description: Ethical, legal, and professional issues in computing: privacy, intellectual property, professional codes of conduct, and social impact of technology.

CMPS205.name: Discrete Structures for Computing
CMPS205.description: Mathematical foundations for computing: logic, set theory, combinatorics, graph theory, and proof techniques used throughout the CS curriculum.

CMPS303.name: Data Structures
CMPS303.description: Core data structures (lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, hash tables) and their implementation, analysis, and application to real problems.

CMPS323.name: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
CMPS323.description: Design and analysis of algorithms: asymptotic complexity, divide-and-conquer, greedy methods, dynamic programming, and NP-completeness.

CMPS351.name: Fundamentals of Database Systems
CMPS351.description: Relational database design, SQL, normalization, transactions, and an introduction to NoSQL and modern data storage systems.

CMPE263.name: Computer Architecture and Organization I
CMPE263.description: Digital logic, CPU architecture, memory hierarchy, instruction sets, and the hardware/software interface.

CMPS310.name: Software Engineering
CMPS310.description: Software development lifecycle, requirements engineering, design patterns, testing, and team-based project methodology.

CMPE355.name: Data Communication and Computer Networks I
CMPE355.description: Network architectures, protocols (TCP/IP), routing, and an introduction to network security, with hands-on configuration labs.

CMPS380.name: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
CMPS380.description: Foundations of cybersecurity: threat modeling, cryptographic basics, secure coding practices, and common vulnerability classes.

CMPS350.name: Web Development Fundamentals
CMPS350.description: Client-server web architecture, HTML/CSS/JavaScript fundamentals, and building dynamic web applications.

CMPS405.name: Operating Systems
CMPS405.description: Operating system concepts: processes, threads, scheduling, memory management, file systems, and concurrency.

CMPS493.name: Senior Project I
CMPS493.notes: Requires minimum 84 CH completed AND CMPS 310 AND (CMPS 350 OR CMPS 405). Alternative: GENG 498.
CMPS493.description: First half of the capstone sequence: team-based project proposal, requirements analysis, and system design under faculty supervision.

CMPS499.name: Senior Project II
CMPS499.description: Second half of the capstone sequence: implementation, testing, and presentation of the senior project developed in CMPS493.

CMPS307.name: Introduction to Project Management and Entrepreneurship
CMPS307.description: Project management fundamentals and entrepreneurship basics for technology ventures, including planning, budgeting, and pitching.

MATH101.name: Calculus I
MATH101.description: Differential and integral calculus of a single variable, limits, derivatives, and their applications.

MATH102.name: Calculus II
MATH102.description: Continuation of Calculus I: integration techniques, sequences and series, and an introduction to multivariable concepts.

MATH231.name: Linear Algebra
MATH231.description: Vector spaces, matrices, linear transformations, eigenvalues/eigenvectors, and applications relevant to computing.

GENG200.name: Probability and Statistics for Engineers
GENG200.description: Probability theory and statistical methods for engineers: distributions, hypothesis testing, and regression.

GENG300.name: Numerical Methods
GENG300.description: Numerical techniques for solving engineering problems: root-finding, interpolation, numerical integration, and differential equations.

PHYS191.name: General Physics for Engineering I
PHYS191.description: Mechanics, motion, forces, and energy for engineering students, with an accompanying lab component.

PHYS192.name: Experimental General Physics for Engineering I
PHYS192.description: Hands-on laboratory experiments accompanying General Physics I: mechanics measurements and data analysis.

PHYS193.name: General Physics for Engineering II
PHYS193.description: Electricity, magnetism, and waves for engineering students, continuing from General Physics I.

PHYS194.name: Experimental General Physics for Engineering II
PHYS194.description: Hands-on laboratory experiments accompanying General Physics II: circuits, fields, and wave phenomena.

CHEM101.name: General Chemistry I
CHEM101.description: General chemistry principles: atomic structure, bonding, stoichiometry, and reaction types relevant to engineering.

CHEM103.name: Experimental General Chemistry I
CHEM103.description: Laboratory experiments accompanying General Chemistry I: safety, measurement, and basic chemical reactions.

ENGL202.name: English Language I (Post Foundation)
ENGL202.description: Academic English composition and communication skills for university-level coursework.

ENGL203.name: English Language II (Post Foundation)
ENGL203.description: Continuation of English Language I with emphasis on research writing, presentations, and critical reading.

ARAB100.name: Arabic Language I
ARAB100.notes: Offered in both Arabic and English
ARAB100.description: Arabic language skills for academic and professional contexts, offered in both Arabic and English.

HIST121.name: History of Qatar
HIST121.notes: Offered in both Arabic and English
HIST121.description: Political, social, and economic history of Qatar from its founding to the present day.

DAWA111.name: Islamic Culture
DAWA111.description: Introduction to Islamic culture, values, and their relevance to contemporary society.

MAGT101.name: Principles of Management
MAGT101.description: Foundational management principles: planning, organizing, leading, and controlling in organizational contexts.

CORE_KS.name: Core Knowledge and Skills Package
CORE_KS.description: University core curriculum package covering general knowledge and transferable skills requirements.

NAT_SCI.name: Natural Science / Mathematics Package
NAT_SCI.description: University core curriculum package satisfying the natural science / mathematics requirement.

HUM_FA.name: Humanities / Fine Arts Package
HUM_FA.description: University core curriculum package covering humanities and fine arts requirements.

SOC_BEH.name: Social and Behavioral Sciences Package
SOC_BEH.description: University core curriculum package covering social and behavioral science requirements.

ELEC1.name: Major Elective I
ELEC1.description: Major elective slot I, filled from the approved CS elective pool (see _elective_pool_reference).

ELEC2.name: Major Elective II
ELEC2.description: Major elective slot II, filled from the approved CS elective pool (see _elective_pool_reference).

ELEC3.name: Major Elective III
ELEC3.description: Major elective slot III, filled from the approved CS elective pool (see _elective_pool_reference).

ELEC4.name: Major Elective IV
ELEC4.description: Major elective slot IV, filled from the approved CS elective pool (see _elective_pool_reference).
